pilot_signal_strength.evidence: M1+IGSD Ortho=0.96 near-orthogonal; three-way Ortho~1.02 (3 seeds); M1+M3 interference confirmed (Ortho=0.41); clear composition taxonomy

hypothesis_survival.evidence: H2 partially falsified (M1+M3 interference); H3 confirmed (IGSD composable); H4 confirmed (task-dependent); H6 refuted (monotonic KL, not inverted-U); IGSD partition no QAS benefit

path_to_full_result.evidence: 14/14 tasks completed; 5 three-way configs x 3 seeds validated; Dream-7B cross-model done; AR comparison done; remaining: full-scale validation + paper writing

novelty.evidence: Novelty 6/10; SlowFast and Fast-dLLM already compose 2 axes; KLASS uses same KL signal; but no 3-axis factorial with quantified Ortho

resource_efficiency.evidence: All 14 tasks on 1-2 GPUs, ~20 hours total; full validation ~14 hours additional

cand_convergence_theory.reason: Core contribution preempted by Chen & Cai (2505.21400) with tight information-theoretic bounds

cand_order_first.reason: LogicDiff achieves +38.7pp on GSM8K, completely superseding the >10pp target; Flexibility Trap identifies the same problem

cand_multigrid.reason: Highest novelty (8/10) but no pilot data; implementation complexity high (feasibility 5/10); if ComposeAccel reveals that KV caching and token selection are highly complementary, multigrid unification becomes a natural follow-up

positive: M1+IGSD near-orthogonal (Ortho=0.91-0.96): KV cache reuse and step reduction are truly orthogonal acceleration axes
Three-way composition stable across 3 seeds (QAS CV < 8.2%) with Ortho~1.02 when M3 guidance disabled
Dream-7B cross-model validation shows 4/5 pattern agreement -- composition patterns are architecture-general
CHR_refine=94.3% empirically confirmed, supporting the M1+IGSD synergy mechanism (frozen tokens create cache-friendly patterns)
Task-dependent recipes confirmed: M3 best for reasoning, IGSD best for speed, M1+IGSD best combined QAS
negative: d2Cache kernel integration yields 0.42x HF baseline (NO_GO); M1 speedup remains projected not measured
M1+M3 shows interference (Ortho=0.41, speedup < 1.0x) -- quality-first composition hypothesis H2 partially falsified
IGSD confidence partitioning provides no QAS benefit (tau=0.0 QAS=1.20 > tau=0.9 QAS=1.03)
KL divergence profile is monotonically increasing, not inverted-U -- H6 refuted
AR baseline dominates DLM by 2x+ in both speed and accuracy
Best three-way speedup is modest (~1.7x at 63% accuracy retention)
Batch sensitivity: composition Ortho degrades from 0.86 (bs=1) to 0.52 (bs=8)
surprising: iter_001 M1+M3 pilot showed Ortho=1.339 (synergy) but iter_002 corrected measurement shows Ortho=0.41 (interference) -- previous result was artifact of flawed metric
IGSD without confidence partition (tau=0.0) outperforms IGSD with partition -- the partition mechanism is harmful
M3 (AR guidance) preserves or improves accuracy on reasoning tasks but causes overall slowdown when composed with M1 (combined speedup < 1.0x)

refinements_for_full_experiments: Reframe IGSD as 'reduced-step decoding' rather than 'speculative denoising with confidence partition'. Acknowledge KLASS explicitly.
Update paper narrative: composition taxonomy and Ortho metric are the main contribution, not headline speedup numbers
Be transparent about M1 implementation gap (d2Cache 0.42x vs projected 1.5-2.5x based on CHR measurements)
Revise H2: M1+M3 interference is a more interesting finding than the original quality-first dominance hypothesis
Frame DLM acceleration as closing DLM-AR gap for users who choose DLMs for bidirectional generation, not competing with AR speed
Report IGSD partition negative result prominently -- it challenges the speculative decoding paradigm for DLMs
next_actions: Run full-scale validation (1319 GSM8K + 500 MATH500, seeds=[42,123,456]) for top 3 three-way configs and M1+IGSD pairwise
Write paper with revised framing: composition taxonomy as main contribution
Include honest AR comparison showing DLM acceleration narrows but does not close the gap
Report negative findings prominently: H6 refuted, IGSD partition harmful, M1+M3 interference
